title: Japan Readies for Decisive World Cup Match Against Tunisia
summary: The Japanese national team, aiming to advance in the tournament, is without the injured Kubo and receives fan support through public events.
source: Yahoo!ニュース・トピックス - 主要

title: Shinkansen Tokaido Services Halted After Accident Involving Person on Tracks
summary: The high-speed line suspended all services after a Nozomi train struck an individual at Hamamatsu Station.
source: Yahoo!ニュース・トピックス - 主要

title: 2026 Kyoto Prize Awarded to Inventor of Perovskite Solar Cells
summary: Japanese scientist Tsutomu Miyasaka, along with American Henry Snaith and artist Laurie Anderson, receive the award.

title: Japanese Vessels Cross Strait of Hormuz After US-Iran Nuclear Deal
summary: All ships with Japanese crew in the Persian Gulf managed to leave the area after the blockade was lifted.

title: Fire at Elementary School in Northern Tokyo Leaves About Ten Injured
summary: The fire, which started in the music room, forced the evacuation of 340 students and left at least 11 people hospitalized.

title: Reform of Japan Post Privatization Law Takes Effect
summary: The new regulation guarantees an annual injection of 65 billion yen to Japan Post to maintain its public functions.
source: Yahoo!ニュース・トピックス - 主要

title: US and Iran Postpone Direct Talks Scheduled for June 19
summary: The meeting in Switzerland was postponed, though both sides maintain their willingness to continue dialogue on the Iranian nuclear program.

title: Price Increases from August: Visas, Snacks, and Automotive Batteries
summary: The government quintuples the visa fee, Koikeya raises the price of its snacks, and GS Yuasa increases lead batteries by 20%.
source: Yahoo!ニュース・トピックス - 主要

title: Arrested for Theft of 15 Million Yen Linked to Murder in Tochigi
summary: The suspect, already arrested a week earlier for the homicide, was accused of stealing a safe in Shinjuku.

title: Labour's Burnham Wins British By-Election and Aspires to Lead Party
summary: Victory in the House of Commons seat strengthens his bid to replace Keir Starmer.

title: Japanese Government Presents Draft Reform of Imperial House Law
summary: The plan to ensure the continuity of the imperial family was submitted to the presidents of both legislative chambers.

title: Suspected Knife on Hokuriku Shinkansen Causes Delays
summary: A passenger reported a man carrying a knife in his backpack, causing the train to stop for 20 minutes.

title: Triple World Title Boxing Match Scheduled for July in Tokyo
summary: Japanese Riku Masuda, ranked number one by the WBA, will seek the champion's belt at the Ryogoku Kokugikan.

title: Fukushima Wins Fourth Honinbo Game and Forces Seventh and Decisive Match
summary: The 7-dan player tied the series 2-2 against champion Ichiriki, bringing the decision to the final match.

title: Portable Speed Radar Stolen on Road in Saitama
summary: The device, weighing about 20 kilograms, was stolen while being used to monitor speed on a national route.

title: Shirai no Taki Waterfall Designated as Place of Scenic Beauty in Japan
summary: The Council for Cultural Affairs approved the distinction for this waterfall, famous for its frozen beauty and praised by historical poets.

title: Nikkei 225 Index Hits New Record Closing High
summary: The main indicator of the Tokyo Stock Exchange rose for a seventh consecutive day, surpassing 71,200 points.

title: Mexico, First Team to Qualify for World Cup Round of 16
summary: The host nation defeated South Korea and secured its second group win, ensuring advancement to the next round.

title: Japanese Senior Officials Make Official Trips to United States and Turkey
summary: Tokyo Governor Yuriko Koike and Defense Minister Minoru Kihara have scheduled overseas visits in July.

title: Japan Commemorates Legacy of Atomic Bomb with Various Initiatives
summary: The Nagasaki victims' association updates its exhibition while the daughter of a physicist who researched atomic radiation gives testimony in the prime minister's hometown.
